etymology.description: 服(服) (fú, "serve") + 務(務) (wù, "duties; affairs") + 員(員) (yuán, "member; person") = a person (員) who serves (服) duties (務) -> waiter/waitress
etymology.interpretation: 服(服) (fú, "serve") implies attending to others' needs. 務(務) (wù, "duties; affairs") emphasizes the responsibilities or tasks involved. 員(員) (yuán, "member; person") specifies someone engaged in a particular role or job. Together, 服務員 (fúwùyuán) represents a person tasked with serving others by performing specific duties, hence a waiter or waitress, focusing on the service aspect in hospitality.

examples.chinese: 服务员正在为我们上菜(服務員正在為我們上菜)。
examples.pinyin: Fúwùyuán zhèngzài wèi wǒmen shàng cài.
examples.english: The waiter is serving us dishes.

examples.chinese: 我叫服务员过来(我叫服務員過來)，然后点了饮料(然後點了飲料)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ jiào fúwùyuán guòlái, ránhòu diǎnle yǐnliào.
examples.english: I called the waiter over and then ordered drinks.

examples.chinese: 服务员给了我们很好的建议(服務員給了我們很好的建議)。
examples.pinyin: Fúwùyuán gěile wǒmen hěn hǎo de jiànyì.
examples.english: The waiter gave us great advice.
